choicesEnglish: I am looking for two keys.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! È il modo più comune per chiedere i biglietti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'Vorrei due biglietti' è la frase corretta.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! It's the most common way to ask for tickets.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'I would like two tickets' is the correct phrase.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! L'impiegato ti chiede l'orario del film.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'Per quale spettacolo?' si riferisce all'orario.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The clerk is asking for the movie time.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'For which showtime?' refers to the time.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Così indichi l'orario scelto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, devi indicare l'orario del film.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! This is how you indicate the chosen time.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you must indicate the movie time.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Chiedi se la sala non è piena.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'Ci sono ancora posti?' chiede della disponibilità.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! You're asking if the room isn't full.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'Are there still seats?' asks about availability.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Significa che sono rimasti pochi posti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'quasi pieno' indica poca disponibilità.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! It means there are few seats left.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'almost full' indicates low availability.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! I posti centrali sono finiti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'posti laterali' sono ai lati della sala.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The central seats are gone.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'side seats' are at the sides of the room.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Ti riferisci alla visuale dei posti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che la posizione non è ideale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! You're referring to the view from the seats.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means the position is not ideal.

choicesEnglish: I'm looking for the back seats.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Per vedere meglio lo schermo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'posti centrali' è la scelta preferita.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! To see the screen better.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'central seats' is the preferred choice.

choicesEnglish: How much do the seats cost?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedi una posizione più centrale o arretrata.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'posti migliori' indica una qualità superiore.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! You're asking for a more central or back position.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'better seats' indicates higher quality.

english: How many tickets do you want?
choicesEnglish: How many tickets do you want?
Which movie do you want to see?
Where do you want to sit?
correctAnswerEnglish: How many tickets do you want?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! È la domanda della cassa.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, l'impiegato chiede il numero di biglietti.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! That's the question from the box office.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the clerk is asking for the number of tickets.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Una risposta semplice ed educata.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'Due biglietti' è quello che ti serve.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! A simple and polite answer.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'Two tickets' is what you need.

english: Is it in the original language?
choicesEnglish: Is it in the original language?
correctAnswerEnglish: Is it in the original language?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedi se le voci sono quelle originali.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'lingua originale' significa non doppiato.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You're asking if the voices are the original ones.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'original language' means not dubbed.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Molti film in lingua originale li hanno.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si riferisce alle scritte tradotte.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Many original language movies have them.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it refers to the translated text.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Significa con le voci italiane.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'doppiato' indica la traduzione dell'audio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! It means with Italian voices.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'dubbed' indicates the audio translation.

italian: Il mio amico non parla italiano.
choicesItalian: Il mio amico non parla italiano.
Il mio amico ama il cinema.
correctAnswerItalian: Il mio amico non parla italiano.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Per questo serve il film in lingua originale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, questa frase spiega la necessità della lingua.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! This is why you need the movie in the original language.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, this phrase explains the need for the language.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! È una decisione di acquisto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'Prendiamo i biglietti' è l'invito a comprare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! It's a purchase decision.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'Let's get the tickets' is the invitation to buy.

english: How much is it in total?
choicesEnglish: How much is it in total?
How much time does it take?
correctAnswerEnglish: How much is it in total?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedi il prezzo complessivo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi il costo totale da pagare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! You're asking for the overall price.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking for the total cost to pay.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Indica il prezzo unitario.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'a biglietto' specifica il prezzo di uno solo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Indicates the unit price.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'per ticket' specifies the price of just one.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! I due modi di pagamento.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, l'impiegato ti chiede come vuoi pagare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! The two payment methods.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the clerk is asking how you want to pay.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Scegli il pagamento elettronico.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'carta' si riferisce alla carta di credito.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You choose electronic payment.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'card' refers to a credit card.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Quando dai la carta o i soldi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'Ecco a lei' si dice consegnando qualcosa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! When you give the card or money.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'Here you go' is said when handing something over.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedi se hai un posto assegnato.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'numerati' significa con un numero specifico.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You're asking if you have an assigned seat.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'numbered' means with a specific number.

italian: Sala tre, in fondo a sinistra.
english: Room three, at the end on the left.
choicesItalian: Sala tre, in fondo a sinistra.
choicesEnglish: Room three, at the end on the left.
Room one, in front on the right.
correctAnswerItalian: Sala tre, in fondo a sinistra.
correctAnswerEnglish: Room three, at the end on the left.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Sono le indicazioni per la sala.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti sta spiegando dove andare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! These are the directions to the room.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's explaining where to go.

english: There's a long line at the box office.
choicesEnglish: There's a long line at the box office.
correctAnswerEnglish: There's a long line at the box office.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Significa che dovrai aspettare.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'fila' indica le persone in attesa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! It means you'll have to wait.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'line' indicates people waiting.

english: We'll be late for the show.
choicesEnglish: We'll be late for the show.
correctAnswerEnglish: We'll be late for the show.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Una preoccupazione comune se c'è fila.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che non arriverai puntuale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A common concern if there's a line.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means you won't arrive on time.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Indica quanto tempo manca.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'tra dieci minuti' indica il futuro prossimo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! It indicates how much time is left.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'in ten minutes' indicates the near future.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Per evitare la fila alla cassa.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'online' significa su internet.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! To avoid the line at the box office.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'online' means on the internet.

english: I'm loading the app on the phone.
choicesEnglish: I'm loading the app on the phone.
correctAnswerEnglish: I'm loading the app on the phone.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Per usare l'applicazione del cinema.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'app' è l'abbreviazione di applicazione.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! To use the cinema's application.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'app' is short for application.

english: It costs one euro in fees.
choicesItalian: Lo sconto è di un euro.
choicesEnglish: It costs one euro in fees.
correctAnswerEnglish: It costs one euro in fees.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un costo extra per la prenotazione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'commissione' è un costo di servizio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! An extra cost for the reservation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'fees' is a service cost.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Il vantaggio di avere il biglietto online.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'saltare' significa non farla.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! The advantage of having an online ticket.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'skip' means not doing it.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! È il tuo biglietto digitale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il codice QR serve per l'ingresso.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! It's your digital ticket.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the QR code is used for entrance.

english: Let's go directly to the entrance.
choicesEnglish: Let's go directly to the entrance.
Let's go to the box office.
correctAnswerEnglish: Let's go directly to the entrance.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Senza passare dalla cassa.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa andare dritto all'entrata.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Without going through the box office.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means going straight to the entrance.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Le coordinate del tuo posto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica la riga e la poltrona.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The coordinates of your seat.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates the row and the chair.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Una posizione scomoda per gli occhi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che la distanza è poca.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! An uncomfortable position for the eyes.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means the distance is short.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Il pubblico si sta accomodando.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'entrando' indica l'azione in corso.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The audience is settling in.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'entering' indicates the action in progress.

choicesEnglish: Shall we get an ice cream?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Una proposta per uno snack.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo se volete la taglia grande.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A proposal for a snack.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking if you want the large size.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Non siete ancora in ritardo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che potete ancora farcela.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! You're not late yet.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means you can still make it.

english: What drinks do you have at the bar?
choicesEnglish: What drinks do you have at the bar?
How much do the drinks cost?
correctAnswerEnglish: What drinks do you have at the bar?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedi la lista delle bevande.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo quali bibite sono disponibili.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You're asking for the list of drinks.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking which drinks are available.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Specifica il contenitore.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'lattina' è il contenitore metallico.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! It specifies the container.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'can' is the metal container.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! I due gusti principali disponibili.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il barista chiede quale gusto preferisci.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! The two main flavors available.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the barista is asking which flavor you prefer.

english: Do you want the large size?
choicesEnglish: Do you want the large size?
Do you want the small size?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do you want the large size?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Il barista chiede la dimensione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si riferisce alla taglia del prodotto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The barista is asking about the size.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it refers to the size of the product.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Per bere comodamente la bibita.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'cannucce' sono i tubicini di plastica o carta.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! To drink your beverage comfortably.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'straws' are the plastic or paper tubes.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Per trasportare tutto in sala.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il vassoio serve per appoggiare popcorn e bibite.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! To carry everything into the room.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the tray is used to hold popcorn and drinks.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedi se la proiezione è in corso.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi se siete arrivati in tempo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You're asking if the screening is in progress.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking if you arrived on time.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Le anteprime prima del film vero e proprio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, i trailer precedono la proiezione principale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The previews before the actual movie.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, trailers precede the main screening.

italian: Non vedo i numeri delle file.
english: I don't see the row numbers.
choicesItalian: Non vedo i numeri delle file.
choicesEnglish: I don't see the row numbers.
correctAnswerItalian: Non vedo i numeri delle file.
correctAnswerEnglish: I don't see the row numbers.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Spesso è difficile trovarli al buio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che non riesci a leggere i numeri.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! It's often hard to find them in the dark.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means you can't read the numbers.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Cerchi la posizione assegnata.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai cercando i posti scritti sul biglietto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You're looking for the assigned position.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're looking for the seats written on the ticket.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Lì di solito si trova il numero del posto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti suggerisce dove cercare il numero.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! That's usually where the seat number is found.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it suggests where to look for the number.

english: I have to turn off my cell phone.
choicesEnglish: I have to turn off my cell phone.
I have to take a photo.
I have to use the internet.
correctAnswerEnglish: I have to turn off my cell phone.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! È buona educazione al cinema.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'spegnere' significa disattivarlo completamente.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! It's good manners at the cinema.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'turn off' means deactivating it completely.

english: I don't want to disturb anyone.
choicesEnglish: I don't want to disturb anyone.
correctAnswerEnglish: I don't want to disturb anyone.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Cerchi di fare silenzio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che vuoi essere rispettoso degli altri.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You're trying to be quiet.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means you want to be respectful of others.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Una reazione comune alla grandezza della sala.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'enorme' significa molto grande.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A common reaction to the size of the room.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'huge' means very large.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Una domanda tipica dopo lo spettacolo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi un parere o un'opinione.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! A typical question after the show.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking for a judgment or opinion.

english: A bit slow at the beginning.
choicesEnglish: A bit slow at the beginning.
correctAnswerEnglish: A bit slow at the beginning.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Esprimi un giudizio sul ritmo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'lento' indica che succede poco.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You're expressing a judgment on the pace.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'slow' indicates that little is happening.

italian: Qual è il tuo genere preferito?
choicesItalian: Qual è il tuo genere preferito?
Chi è il tuo attore preferito?
correctAnswerItalian: Qual è il tuo genere preferito?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedi il tipo di film che preferisce.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'genere' si riferisce alla categoria del film.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! You're asking for the type of movie they prefer.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'genre' refers to the movie's category.

choicesEnglish: I want to see a horror.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Esprimi la tua preferenza.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'commedie' sono film fatti per ridere.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! You're expressing your preference.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'comedies' are movies made to make you laugh.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Significa che non hai sentito o capito dei dialoghi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'battute' sono le frasi dette dagli attori.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! It means you didn't hear or understand some dialogues.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'lines' (battute) are the sentences spoken by the actors.

english: Shall we go get a pizza?
choicesEnglish: Shall we go get a pizza?
Shall we go back to the cinema?
correctAnswerEnglish: Shall we go get a pizza?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Una proposta per dopo il film.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai invitando l'altro a mangiare fuori.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A proposal for after the movie.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're inviting the other person to eat out.

italian: Ho un problema con il biglietto.
english: I have a problem with the ticket.
choicesItalian: Ho un problema con il biglietto.
choicesEnglish: I have a problem with the ticket.
I want to change the ticket.
correctAnswerItalian: Ho un problema con il biglietto.
correctAnswerEnglish: I have a problem with the ticket.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Per segnalare qualcosa che non va.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, questa frase introduce una difficoltà.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! To report something that isn't working.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, this phrase introduces a difficulty.

english: The QR code is not working.
choicesEnglish: The QR code is not working.
correctAnswerEnglish: The QR code is not working.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Un tipico problema tecnico all'ingresso.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che il lettore non lo riconosce.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! A typical technical problem at the entrance.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means the reader doesn't recognize it.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un consiglio per risolvere il problema.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'personale' sono le persone che lavorano lì.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A tip to solve the problem.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'staff' are the people who work there.
